questions.question: Explain the concept of checksum in data communication. How does it help in error detection?
keyPoints: Checksum is a computed value based on data content
Used to verify integrity during transmission
Sender computes, receiver recomputes and compares
Mismatch indicates error in data

questions.question: Describe the Internet checksum algorithm as per RFC 1071.
keyPoints: Data divided into 16-bit words
All words added using one's complement arithmetic
Carry overflow added back (end-around carry)
Final one's complement of sum is checksum

questions.question: What is one's complement arithmetic? How is it different from two's complement?
keyPoints: Negative numbers represented by inverting all bits
Has two zeros: +0 (0000H) and -0 (FFFFH)
Two's complement: invert and add 1
Two's complement has single zero representation

questions.question: Explain how checksum verification is done at the receiver end.
keyPoints: Receiver includes checksum field in addition
Sums all 16-bit words including checksum
If result is all 1s (FFFFH), no error detected
If any 0 bit found, error is present

questions.question: Why is checksum not included in IPv6 header? What is the rationale?
keyPoints: IPv6 assumes reliable link-layer error detection
Processing checksum at each router in IPv4 adds overhead
All IPv6 nodes have strong link-layer error detection
Optional in IPv4, removed in IPv6 for performance

questions.question: Compare checksum with CRC (Cyclic Redundancy Check) in terms of error detection capability.
keyPoints: CRC detects more burst errors than checksum
CRC uses polynomial division, checksum uses modular addition
Both can detect single-bit errors reliably
Checksum is simpler to implement but less robust than CRC

questions.question: Explain the terms: end-around carry, ones complement, and weighted checksum.
keyPoints: End-around carry: overflow bit added back to LSB in one's complement
One's complement: bit inversion to represent negative numbers
Weighted checksum: different weights for different positions for better burst error detection
